title: rubus corylifolius subsp. serrulatus
intro: Rubus corylifolius subsp. serrulatus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan wild raspberry, is a species of bramble native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus corylifolius subsp. serrulatus include its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves, stems, and roots in hot water or using them as a tincture to reap its medicinal benefits.
